requirements: Explain the knowledge-transfer job without reducing it to summarization.
Explain levels, interactive fallback, and authority boundaries.
Distinguish real dogfood from controlled evaluation.
Explain five development versus three held-out cases.
Explain packaging, semantic, and renderer comparisons.
State what happens next and what remains unpromoted.

readerQuestions.expected: It transfers the smallest accurate, usable mental model for a reader while preserving facts, uncertainty, boundaries, and proof limits.

readerQuestions.q: Why are real-work testing and frozen-case evaluation both needed?
readerQuestions.expected: Real work measures usefulness and experience; frozen cases isolate whether comprehension improves without factual, proportionality, or authority regressions.

readerQuestions.q: What is the difference between development and held-out cases?
readerQuestions.expected: The five development cases may guide tuning; the three held-outs stay untouched until final evaluation so they test generalization.
Implementation, posting, publication, deployment, provider or shared-data mutation, default Coordinator promotion, and public release.

humanChecksPending: Whether the single visual materially helped
Whether the document is too long, too short, or missing a decision-relevant boundary
